stories.title: US offers $3M bounty for financial information on Haitian gangs
stories.description: The reward targets financial networks of Viv Ansanm and Gran Grif, shifting tactics from previous bounties focused on individual gang leaders.

stories.title: DOJ admits ICE arrested immigrants at courts under wrong legal authority
stories.description: U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton said the government's defense of immigration courthouse arrests was based on a memo that only applied to criminal courthouses.

stories.title: Iran claims missile strike on USS Abraham Lincoln as Gulf war widens
stories.description: The IRGC launched attacks on Israel and U.S. bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain as drones set fire to a fuel tank at Kuwait's airport.

stories.title: Zelensky says US tied security guarantees to Ukraine ceding Donbas
stories.description: In a Reuters interview, the Ukrainian president warned the conditional demand would compromise the security of Ukraine and Europe.

stories.title: Senate GOP launches reconciliation bill for Iran war, ICE and voter ID
stories.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham said his Budget Committee will "expeditiously" draft a second party-line bill to bypass the filibuster, but key Republicans are skeptical.

stories.title: Six Arab states demand Iraq halt militia attacks from its territory
stories.description: Baghdad summoned the U.S. envoy and vowed a UN complaint after a deadly airstrike on the Habbaniyah base killed seven soldiers.

stories.title: Pew poll finds 61% of Americans disapprove of Trump's Iran war
stories.description: The survey of 3,524 adults, conducted March 16-22, also found 59% believe the U.S. made the wrong decision to use military force against Tehran.

stories.title: US and Israel temporarily spare two Iranian leaders to allow peace talks
stories.description: Ghalibaf and Araghchi will not be targeted for four to five days as mediators push for ceasefire negotiations amid the ongoing conflict.

stories.title: Iran says it now controls Strait of Hormuz transit rules permanently
stories.description: Tehran told the UN that only "non-hostile" ships may pass through the waterway carrying one-fifth of global oil, barring U.S. and Israeli vessels.

stories.title: White House rejects Musk's offer to pay TSA workers
stories.description: The rejection came as the DHS shutdown hit day 40, with a TSA official telling Congress that wait times have reached historic highs.

stories.title: JetBlue shares surge on report it hired advisers to explore sale
stories.description: The struggling airline is reportedly evaluating potential deals with United Airlines, Alaska Airlines, or Southwest Airlines.

stories.title: Iranian analyst warns Tehran ready to seize UAE, Bahrain coastlines
stories.description: Morteza Simiari said on state TV that Iran's military has rehearsed coastal operations and will act if the U.S. "makes any mistake."

stories.title: White House says congressional approval not needed for Iran strikes
stories.description: Spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. postponed strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure after "productive conversations" but warned Trump is "prepared to unleash hell."

stories.title: Think tank proposes $100K cap on Social Security benefits
stories.description: The Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget's "Six Figure Limit" plan would trim payouts to wealthy retirees as the program faces insolvency by 2032.

stories.title: Egypt offers to host Iran-US talks as Tehran rejects ceasefire plan
stories.description: Foreign Minister Abdelatty called direct engagement between Washington and Tehran "the last opportunity" to avoid full-scale regional escalation.

stories.title: Netanyahu orders 48-hour blitz on Iran amid ceasefire fears
stories.description: Israel's PM directed commanders to destroy as much of Iran's weapons industry as possible before a potential U.S.-brokered ceasefire deal.

stories.title: NASA climate scientist Kate Marvel quits, citing Trump's 'attacks on science'
stories.description: Marvel said a year of funding uncertainty and dismantled programs left her unable to do meaningful work at the Goddard Institute for Space Studies.

stories.title: TSA chief warns of airport closures as DHS shutdown hits Day 40
stories.description: Acting administrator told Congress that 480 officers have quit, wait times have topped four hours, and nearly $1 billion in paychecks have been missed.

stories.title: Israel hits Tehran naval research center, completes waves of strikes
stories.description: Defense Minister Katz says Israel has dropped over 15,000 munitions on Iran since the war began on Feb. 28, four times last June's total.

stories.title: Pentagon signs deals to quadruple missile output amid Iran war
stories.description: Lockheed Martin, BAE Systems, and Honeywell Aerospace agreed to accelerate production of key weapons as the U.S. replenishes wartime stockpiles.

stories.title: Trump sets May 14-15 for rescheduled Beijing summit with Xi
stories.description: The White House announced firm dates for the postponed China trip, which was delayed by the ongoing U.S. and Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: Fed's operating losses fell to $19B in 2025 from $78B
stories.description: The central bank's audited financial statements show interest expenses dropped sharply as rate cuts reduced what the Fed pays on bank reserves.

stories.title: EPA waives summer smog rules on gasoline amid Iran war price surge
stories.description: The emergency waiver allows E15 ethanol-blend fuel sales starting May 1, as the Strait of Hormuz closure drives gas prices sharply higher.

stories.title: Iran bolsters defenses on Kharg Island ahead of possible US assault
stories.description: Tehran has deployed MANPADs, troops, and anti-armor mines to the strategic oil hub as the Trump administration weighs a ground operation, CNN reports.

stories.title: Meta, YouTube found negligent in social media addiction trial
stories.description: A Los Angeles jury awarded $3 million in damages to a young woman who alleged the platforms' addictive design features caused her mental health struggles.

stories.title: Indiana bars Piece by Piece Autism Centers from Medicaid
stories.description: The provider collected $29 million in 2023 to treat just 84 patients by exploiting a billing loophole that allowed reimbursements of up to $640 per hour.

stories.title: Iran rejects U.S. ceasefire plan, issues five demands
stories.description: Tehran dismissed Washington's 15-point proposal as "excessive and disconnected from reality" and countered with its own conditions to end the war.

stories.title: Supreme Court rules ISPs not liable for users' piracy
stories.description: The unanimous decision reverses a jury verdict against Cox Communications in a lawsuit led by Sony Music over illegal downloads of more than 10,000 songs.

stories.title: TSA chief tells Congress agency won't be ready for World Cup
stories.description: Nearly 460 officers have quit and unpaid payroll nears $1 billion as the DHS shutdown enters its 39th day, the acting TSA administrator testified.

stories.title: Iran signals preference for Vance as US negotiator, rejecting Witkoff and Kushner
stories.description: Tehran conveyed through back channels that it views Vance as more inclined to end the war, citing a "deficit of trust" with prior negotiators.

stories.title: US gas prices hit $3.98 as lawmakers push tax suspensions
stories.description: The national average has surged more than $1 in under a month, driven by the Iran conflict, with Georgia suspending its gas tax and Congress weighing a federal pause.

stories.title: Trump gets daily strike montage on Iran war, raising concerns
stories.description: Officials told NBC News the curated two-minute videos emphasize U.S. successes while offering little detail on Iranian attacks or setbacks.

stories.title: NASA confirms Artemis II is go for April 1 moon launch
stories.description: Four astronauts are set to become the first humans to travel to the moon in over 50 years aboard a 10-day test flight.

stories.title: Pentagon orders 82nd Airborne troops to Middle East amid Iran ceasefire push
stories.description: The deployment of up to 3,000 paratroopers coincides with a 15-point U.S. ceasefire proposal sent to Tehran through Pakistani intermediaries.

stories.title: US ships 30,000 barrels of fuel to Cuba's private sector
stories.description: The Trump administration's dual strategy of blockading Cuba's government while supplying private businesses is gaining traction, according to Reuters.

stories.title: Astronomers spot two planets forming around a young star like our sun
stories.description: WISPIT 2 is only the second known system where two planets have been directly observed taking shape, offering a rare window into our solar system's origins.

stories.title: New York Times accuses Pentagon of defying court order on press access
stories.description: The paper filed a new motion Tuesday after the department closed its press corridor and imposed escort requirements days after a judge struck down its media policy.

stories.title: Prosecutor admitted no crime evidence in DOJ probe of Fed's Powell
stories.description: A newly unsealed transcript shows the lead prosecutor told a judge his office had no evidence of criminal wrongdoing by the Fed chair.

stories.title: Senate Republicans block 9th TSA funding bid as shutdown nears 40 days
stories.description: Travelers face hours-long waits at major airports as over 50,000 TSA officers work without pay during the DHS funding standoff.

stories.title: US Army raises max enlistment age to 42, eases marijuana rules
stories.description: Updated regulation published March 20 also drops the waiver requirement for a single marijuana possession conviction to speed up recruiting.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike hits Christian town north of Beirut for first time
stories.description: The strike on Sahel Alma marks a widening of Israel's air campaign into previously untouched areas of Lebanon amid the escalating war with Hezbollah.

stories.title: SpaceX aims to file for record-setting IPO in the next two weeks
stories.description: The rocket and satellite company is moving toward what could be the largest public offering in history, with a June listing as the target.

stories.title: US assessing how to resume nuclear weapons testing
stories.description: Undersecretary DiNanno told lawmakers the administration has made no decisions on a testing program but did not rule out underground explosive tests.

stories.title: DHS shutdown delayed NTSB investigators probing deadly LaGuardia crash
stories.description: NTSB Chair Jennifer Homendy said investigators waited hours in TSA lines, preventing the team from completing a full first day of work at the crash site.

stories.title: Democrat flips Florida House seat in Trump's Mar-a-Lago district
stories.description: Emily Gregory defeated Trump-endorsed Republican Jon Maples in a district the president carried by about 10 points in 2024.

stories.title: US airstrikes kill 15 Iraq PMF fighters, including Anbar commander
stories.description: The deadliest single strike since the US-Israeli war on Iran began also targeted PMF leader Falih al-Fayadh's residence in a separate attack.

stories.title: Iran charges ships up to $2M for Hormuz passage
stories.description: Some vessels have already paid the ad hoc fees, according to Bloomberg, as shipping through the strait remains near collapse amid the Iran war.

stories.title: Senate blocks third Democratic bid to curb Trump's Iran war powers
stories.description: The 47-53 vote marked the third time this month Republicans defeated a resolution demanding congressional authorization for the military campaign.

stories.title: Netanyahu taps top aide Dermer to vet US-Iran deal
stories.description: Ron Dermer's mission focuses on ensuring Iran's nuclear program is dismantled and its enriched uranium stockpile eliminated, according to Bloomberg.

stories.title: Anduril, Palantir building Golden Dome missile shield software
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reported the two firms are part of a nine-company consortium developing the $185 billion antimissile system's command-and-control layer.

stories.title: Denmark's left bloc wins election but falls short of majority
stories.description: Centrist leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen emerges as kingmaker with 14 seats as weeks of coalition talks loom.

stories.title: Death toll rises to 69 in Colombia military plane crash
stories.description: A C-130 Hercules went down shortly after takeoff Monday in the Amazon jungle, marking one of Colombia's deadliest military air disasters.

stories.title: Iran says drones struck Israeli defense sites, Ben Gurion Airport
stories.description: Tuesday's claimed strikes targeted Rafael Advanced Defense Systems and Israel Aerospace Industries as Operation True Promise 4 enters its fourth week.

stories.title: Judge orders Trump to unfreeze $3B in Chicago transit funds
stories.description: A federal judge granted a temporary restraining order releasing frozen rail funding, calling the administration's selective enforcement "pretextual."

stories.title: Macron urges Iran to negotiate in third call with Pezeshkian since war began
stories.description: The French president demanded an end to "unacceptable" attacks and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as U.S. peace efforts intensify.

stories.title: Iranian missile intercepted over Lebanon in first since regional war began
stories.description: Lebanon also expelled Iran's ambassador-designate, ordering him to leave by March 29 after accusing Tehran of violating diplomatic norms.

stories.title: Iran stops all gas exports to Turkey after South Pars strike
stories.description: The halt follows Israel's March 18 attack on the world's largest natural gas field, cutting off about 14% of Turkey's supply.

stories.title: Lebanon expels Iran's ambassador as Israel vows to occupy southern Lebanon
stories.description: Beirut declared Tehran's envoy persona non grata on Tuesday as Israel announced plans to seize territory up to the Litani River.

stories.title: Debris from intercepted missiles knocks out 7 Kuwait power lines
stories.description: Kuwait's Defense Ministry reported intercepting 17 ballistic missiles and 13 drones in 24 hours as the country endures its worst sustained bombardment since the Iran war began.

stories.title: Israel says it killed Quds Force operative in Beirut strike
stories.description: The IDF and Shin Bet said a naval strike on Monday killed Muhammad Ali Kourani, who they described as an Iranian-directed operative planning attacks from Lebanon.

stories.title: China presses Iran to start peace talks as Gulf war escalates
stories.description: Wang Yi told counterpart Abbas Araghchi that "talking is always better than fighting," urging all sides to seize "every opportunity" for negotiations.

stories.title: British troops down 14 drones in record night at Iraq base
stories.description: RAF Regiment forces intercepted the largest single-night drone swarm since the Iran conflict began, as Tehran resumed attacks after an Eid lull.

stories.title: Second projectile hits Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant in a week
stories.description: Iran accused the U.S. and Israel of striking the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant on Tuesday, the second such incident since March 17.

stories.title: Democrats reject GOP offer to end DHS shutdown, demand ICE reforms
stories.description: Senate Republicans proposed funding 94% of the DHS budget, but Democrats said the plan lacks immigration enforcement changes they have required since January.

stories.title: Rubio testifies at ex-roommate's secret Venezuela lobbying trial
stories.description: The secretary of state became the first sitting Cabinet member to take the stand in a criminal trial since 1983.

stories.title: Erdogan vows to keep Turkey out of Iran war's 'ring of fire'
stories.description: On the 25th day of the US-Israeli air campaign against Iran, Turkey's president blamed Netanyahu and warned against traps to draw Ankara into the conflict.

stories.title: Moldova declares 60-day energy emergency after Russian strikes sever key power line
stories.description: Parliament voted 72-0 to impose the emergency after overnight drone strikes on Ukraine knocked out the line supplying up to 70% of Moldova's electricity.

stories.title: NASA outlines moon strategy as Artemis II targets April launch
stories.description: The agency is hosting a Washington event to detail how it plans to return astronauts to the lunar surface by 2028 despite ongoing delays.

stories.title: Iran fires cluster bomb missile at Bnei Brak, injuring 9
stories.description: Multiple Iranian missile waves struck Israeli cities on Tuesday as the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran entered its fourth week.

stories.title: Saudi crown prince urges Trump to press for Iran regime's fall
stories.description: Mohammed bin Salman has called the US-Israeli war a "historic chance" to reshape the Middle East as Gulf states edge toward open confrontation with Tehran.

stories.title: Aid flotilla docks in Havana as US oil blockade deepens Cuba's crisis
stories.description: The first vessel of the "Our America Convoy" delivered medical supplies, food, and solar panels, with two more ships expected this week.

stories.title: CENTCOM says 9,000 targets hit in Iran campaign's first 24 days
stories.description: Iran has escalated missile and drone attacks against Gulf states even as U.S. officials say Tehran's combat capability is in steep decline.

stories.title: Israel renews Beirut bombardment, killing 3
stories.description: Airstrikes hit a residential building in Bchamoun and seven locations across Beirut's southern suburbs after a two-day lull in attacks.

stories.title: Modi, Trump stress keeping Strait of Hormuz open amid Iran war
stories.description: Their first call since the Feb. 28 U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran focused on de-escalation and securing the vital oil shipping route.

stories.title: Brazil court places Bolsonaro under house arrest on health grounds
stories.description: Justice Alexandre de Moraes reversed months of denials, granting a 90-day house arrest after the ex-president was hospitalized with severe pneumonia.

stories.title: Hezbollah's missile stockpile down to one-sixth of pre-war levels, Israel says
stories.description: Israel's military estimates the Iranian-backed group retains between 11,000 and 13,000 missiles and rockets, down from roughly 75,000 before October 2023.

stories.title: Iran war drives fertilizer costs higher as U.S. planting season nears
stories.description: Surging nitrogen prices, supplier shortages, and Strait of Hormuz disruptions are compounding years of losses for American grain farmers.

stories.title: NASA cancels Lunar Gateway, will build $20B moon base
stories.description: Administrator Jared Isaacman said the agency will repurpose the orbital station's components for a permanent lunar surface base over seven years.

stories.title: Iran toughens negotiation stance, presses US for major concessions
stories.description: Tehran's Revolutionary Guards are driving a harder negotiating line, with senior sources saying Iran will refuse to discuss limits on its missile program.

stories.title: Pilots warned of LaGuardia hazards before deadly crash, CNN review finds
stories.description: A CNN review of two years of safety reports revealed repeated warnings about miscommunication and near-misses at the airport before Sunday's fatal collision.

stories.title: EU delays proposal to permanently ban Russian oil imports
stories.description: The European Commission cited "current geopolitical developments" as the Iran war roils global energy markets with crude prices above $100 a barrel.

stories.title: Iran war oil crisis revives US renewable energy debate
stories.description: The largest oil supply disruption in history has Democrats pushing clean energy legislation as gas prices top $3.70 a gallon.

stories.title: Iran fears ceasefire talks are a trap to kill its remaining leaders
stories.description: Tehran suspects face-to-face negotiations could be used to target parliament speaker Ghalibaf, one of few surviving senior officials.

stories.title: Taliban frees American academic Dennis Coyle after over a year in detention
stories.description: Coyle, a 64-year-old linguist held in near-solitary confinement since January 2025, was reunited with his family in Kabul on Eid al-Fitr.

stories.title: Russia launches 16 Rassvet satellites to rival Starlink
stories.description: Bureau 1440 deployed its first operational batch from Plesetsk Cosmodrome on March 23, targeting commercial broadband service by 2027.

stories.title: Israel to occupy southern Lebanon up to Litani River, defence minister says
stories.description: Defense Minister Katz declared Israel will seize a "defensive buffer" covering nearly a tenth of Lebanon, the first explicit statement of such intent.

stories.title: Gallup: Only 28% of US workers say it's a good time to find a quality job
stories.description: For the first time in Gallup's tracking, more American workers report struggling in their lives than thriving, as job market confidence hits a multiyear low.

stories.title: China mapping ocean floor for submarine warfare, Reuters finds
stories.description: A Reuters investigation tracked 42 Chinese research vessels systematically charting seabeds across the Pacific, Indian, and Arctic oceans over five years.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia, UAE edge toward joining war on Iran
stories.description: Riyadh opened King Fahd Air Base to U.S. forces while the UAE dismantled Iranian institutions in Dubai, the Wall Street Journal reported.

stories.title: Sunken Soviet sub is leaking radioactive material, study finds
stories.description: A new PNAS study confirms the Komsomolets submarine is releasing strontium, cesium, uranium, and plutonium into the Norwegian Sea from its corroding hull.

stories.title: Magnitude 7.6 earthquake strikes near Tonga; no tsunami threat
stories.description: The deep quake hit 153 km west of Neiafu on Tuesday with no immediate reports of damage or casualties, according to USGS.

stories.title: AWS Bahrain region disrupted by drones for second time this month
stories.description: Amazon confirmed drone activity knocked out its cloud computing region in Bahrain again, as the company urges customers to migrate workloads elsewhere.

stories.title: ISS images of Russian missile strike on Kyiv verified by analysts
stories.description: Photographs taken from the space station on Dec. 27 show ballistic missiles streaking toward Kyiv and air defense systems firing back.

stories.title: Kalshi, Polymarket ban insider trading as senators target industry
stories.description: The two largest prediction market platforms rushed to announce new guardrails after bipartisan legislation threatened to ban sports contracts.

stories.title: Pentagon closes press workspace, moves reporters off-site after court loss
stories.description: Three days after a judge ruled its press restrictions unconstitutional, the Defense Department is relocating journalists to an annex outside the building.

stories.title: Senate confirms Mullin as homeland security secretary
stories.description: The Oklahoma Republican replaces Kristi Noem and inherits a department partially shut down since mid-February over immigration enforcement disputes.

stories.title: Judge appoints career prosecutor as NJ's US attorney
stories.description: Robert Frazer's selection resolves a bitter fight between the judiciary and the Trump administration over control of the federal prosecution office.

stories.title: Israel used Iran's own street cameras to track and kill Khamenei
stories.description: An AP investigation details how Israeli intelligence hacked nearly all of Tehran's traffic cameras years ago, turning Iran's surveillance network into a targeting tool.

stories.title: Jury awards $19.25M to woman who accused Cosby of 1972 assault
stories.description: The verdict in Santa Monica is the largest civil judgment against the 88-year-old entertainer, who maintains any encounter was consensual.

stories.title: China urges US, Israel to halt Middle East fighting, warns of 'vicious cycle'
stories.description: Beijing's diplomatic push comes as Trump postpones threatened strikes on Iranian power plants for five days amid disputed peace talks with Tehran.

stories.title: Toyota invests $1B in Kentucky, Indiana plants
stories.description: The funding will prepare its Georgetown factory for a second battery electric vehicle and boost output of the Camry, RAV4 and Grand Highlander.

stories.title: Russia and Vietnam sign deal to build Vietnam's first nuclear plant
stories.description: The Ninh Thuan 1 plant, featuring two reactors with up to 1.2 gigawatts each, is expected to be operational after 2031.

stories.title: Trump admin pays TotalEnergies $928M to exit offshore wind
stories.description: The deal cancels two East Coast wind leases and redirects the funds into LNG and oil production, marking a new tactic after courts blocked project suspensions.

stories.title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to take effect provisionally May 1
stories.description: The European Commission announced the provisional start date for the landmark pact, which will eliminate tariffs on over 90% of trade between the two blocs.

stories.title: Both pilots killed as jet hits fire truck at LaGuardia
stories.description: Air traffic control audio reveals a controller frantically shouting "stop" before an Air Canada Express plane struck a fire truck on the runway.

stories.title: Trump tells ICE agents to drop masks at airports as deployment begins
stories.description: ICE agents arrived at 14 major U.S. airports Monday to ease TSA staffing shortages caused by the five-week partial DHS shutdown.

stories.title: Wright calls oil disruptions 'temporary,' urges producers to boost output
stories.description: At CERAWeek, the energy secretary said prices haven't risen enough to curb demand as the U.S. ramps up its historic SPR drawdown.

stories.title: Newark Airport grounds flights after smoke forces tower evacuation
stories.description: An elevator malfunction caused a burning smell that led controllers to evacuate to a backup facility, halting arrivals and departures.

stories.title: Sinopec says it won't buy Iranian crude despite US sanctions waiver
stories.description: China's largest refiner is cutting runs by 5% and pushing Beijing to release state oil reserves as the Strait of Hormuz crisis squeezes supply.

stories.title: Zelensky warns Patriot missile deficit is inevitable as Iran war drains stockpiles
stories.description: The Ukrainian president said 803 interceptors were used on the first day of the Middle East war — more than the U.S. produces in a year.

stories.title: Supreme Court hears case that could end mail ballot grace periods in 14-plus states
stories.description: Justices weighed whether federal law bars states from counting mail ballots that arrive after Election Day, with a ruling expected by summer.

stories.title: IAEA chief says no war can end Iran's nuclear program
stories.description: Rafael Grossi told CBS News that Iran retains the knowledge and capacity to restart enrichment despite extensive U.S.-Israeli strikes.

stories.title: WMO says Earth's climate 'more out of balance' than ever
stories.description: The agency's annual report finds 2015-2025 was the hottest 11-year stretch on record, with energy imbalance at a 65-year high.

stories.title: Bessent defends easing Russia, Iran oil sanctions on Meet the Press
stories.description: Treasury Secretary said Russia would gain at most $2 billion from the waiver — or face $150-per-barrel oil prices without it.

stories.title: Cuba says its military is preparing for possible U.S. aggression
stories.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Carlos Fernandez de Cossio said regime change is "absolutely" off the table despite Trump's talk of "taking" the island.

stories.title: Mossad plan to spark Iran uprising stalls three weeks into war
stories.description: Intelligence chief Barnea promised Netanyahu that Israeli operations could trigger regime-toppling unrest, but no uprising has materialized.

stories.title: Zelenskyy demands tighter Russia sanctions as Florida peace talks enter day two
stories.description: Ukraine's president cited 1,550 drone strikes in one week, saying eased oil sanctions fund Russia's war as bilateral talks with the U.S. continue.

stories.title: Trump draws bipartisan rebuke for celebrating Mueller's death
stories.description: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director and special counsel in the Trump-Russia probe, died Friday at 81. Trump said he was "glad" on Truth Social.

stories.title: IAEA says no radiation leaks after strikes near nuclear sites in Iran, Israel
stories.description: Tit-for-tat attacks hit near Natanz and Dimona on day 23 of the war, with IAEA chief Grossi urging "maximum military restraint."

stories.title: Senate blocks transgender sports ban amendment to voting bill
stories.description: The 49-41 party-line vote rejected a GOP push to attach the ban to the SAVE America Act during a rare weekend session.

stories.title: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in March as US oil blockade tightens
stories.description: The grid collapse left millions without power as aging infrastructure buckles under fuel shortages caused by the Trump administration's economic pressure campaign.

stories.title: US envoy calls Miami talks with Ukraine 'constructive' amid deadly attacks
stories.description: Special Envoy Steve Witkoff said discussions with Ukrainian officials focused on narrowing gaps toward a peace deal as both sides traded fire Saturday.

stories.title: G7 demands Iran halt attacks, vows to protect energy supplies
stories.description: The joint statement condemned strikes across eight Middle Eastern nations and backed the right of targeted countries to defend themselves.

stories.title: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel, dies at 81
stories.description: Mueller reshaped the FBI after 9/11 and led the investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election during Trump's first term.

stories.title: US quietly lifts sanctions on Russian nationals in fourth wave this month
stories.description: The Treasury Department removed two Russian citizens and several other entities from its sanctions list on March 20, offering no explanation.

stories.title: UK expands US base access to strike Iran's Strait of Hormuz missile sites
stories.description: Starmer had initially limited US use of British bases to defensive measures but broadened the terms Friday to include protecting commercial shipping.

stories.title: Indian, Asian refiners move to buy Iranian oil after US sanctions waiver
stories.description: The 30-day exemption covers roughly 170 million barrels of Iranian crude already at sea, the third such waiver in two weeks.

stories.title: Democrats conduct informal vote counts on ousting Schumer
stories.description: Sen. Chris Murphy revealed that Democratic lawmakers have tallied votes on removing Schumer as minority leader, though he said Schumer retains enough support to stay.

stories.title: Israel launches new wave of strikes on Beirut targeting Hezbollah
stories.description: Saturday's airstrikes follow evacuation warnings as the ground offensive in southern Lebanon widens into its third week.

stories.title: Switzerland halts new arms exports to US over Iran war
stories.description: Bern cited its neutrality obligations, saying no new weapons licenses have been issued to Washington since the conflict began Feb. 28.

stories.title: Judge blocks Pentagon policy restricting press access
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sided with The New York Times, ruling that key parts of the credentialing restrictions imposed under Defense Secretary Hegseth are illegal.

stories.title: Jury finds Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors
stories.description: A San Francisco jury found that two of Musk's tweets during his 2022 Twitter acquisition constituted securities fraud, with damages likely in the billions.

stories.title: Iran offers to let Japanese ships pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran is ready to facilitate transit for Japanese vessels if Tokyo coordinates with Iran, as negotiations continue.

stories.title: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia base
stories.description: Tehran launched two IRBMs at the joint U.S.-U.K. Indian Ocean base, marking its first known attempt to strike a target roughly 2,500 miles away.

stories.title: Hegseth faces backlash for invoking faith in Iran briefings
stories.description: The defense secretary's explicitly Christian rhetoric at Pentagon briefings has drawn sharp criticism as the U.S. enters its third week of war with Iran.

stories.title: Trump ally asked ICE to detain his ex in custody fight, NYT reports
stories.description: Paolo Zampolli contacted a senior ICE official to have his Brazilian ex-girlfriend detained and deported, aiding his bid for custody of their son.

stories.title: U.S. prosecutors open criminal probes into Colombia's President
stories.description: Federal offices in Manhattan and Brooklyn are examining whether Petro met with drug traffickers or received narcotics-linked campaign donations.

stories.title: Pentagon rushes Marines, warships to Middle East as Iran war widens
stories.description: The USS Boxer group and about 8,000 additional service members are headed to the region as the U.S. weighs further operations, including along Iran's coastline.

stories.title: Amazon developing AI-powered smartphone a decade after Fire Phone flop
stories.description: The project, codenamed "Transformer," would center on Alexa and AI-driven interactions rather than competing on hardware specs with Apple and Samsung.

stories.title: Trump privately concedes deportation push went too far, WSJ reports
stories.description: The president directed aides to rebrand the effort around arresting "bad guys" as polls show most Americans think enforcement has gone too far.

stories.title: US launches air campaign to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Warplanes and attack helicopters are targeting Iranian naval assets as daily shipping traffic through the vital energy corridor has dropped roughly 95%.

stories.title: Old-growth forests store far more carbon than logged ones, study finds
stories.description: A decade-long study published in Science reveals Sweden's primary forests hold 83% more carbon than managed woodlands, driven largely by overlooked soil reserves.

stories.title: US military readies Guantanamo Bay camp for potential Cuban exodus
stories.description: DeSantis warned of mass migration to Florida as the SOUTHCOM commander told Congress the military has plans to handle refugees at Guantanamo Bay.

stories.title: Denmark planned to destroy Greenland runways if US invaded
stories.description: Danish broadcaster DR revealed that troops deployed to Greenland in January carried explosives to sabotage airstrips, with orders to fight if the U.S. attacked.

stories.title: DOJ sues Harvard over alleged antisemitism on campus
stories.description: The lawsuit seeks billions in taxpayer funds back and accuses the university of being "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students.

stories.title: Alibaba, Tencent shed $66 billion as AI plans fail to convince investors
stories.description: China's two largest tech companies lost a combined $66 billion in market value in roughly 24 hours after disappointing earnings and costly AI spending plans.

stories.title: US bars Cuba, North Korea from Russia oil sanctions waiver
stories.description: OFAC amended its temporary Russian oil license after a tanker reportedly delivered diesel to fuel-starved Cuba using deceptive shipping practices.

stories.title: EU leaders endorse tax cuts, ETS overhaul to fight energy price surge
stories.description: Von der Leyen pledged "temporary, tailored, and targeted" relief as the bloc grapples with soaring costs tied to the war in Iran.

stories.title: Live Nation CEO admits pay tied to 'solving' DOJ antitrust case
stories.description: Michael Rapino testified Thursday that about 90% of his compensation depends on profit targets and resolving the federal government's antitrust challenge.

stories.title: Sailor's Strava jog exposes French carrier's location amid Iran conflict
stories.description: Le Monde revealed that a naval officer's public fitness data pinpointed the Charles de Gaulle near Cyprus during its eastern Mediterranean deployment.

top.title: US offers $3M bounty for financial information on Haitian gangs
top.description: The reward targets financial networks of Viv Ansanm and Gran Grif, shifting tactics from previous bounties focused on individual gang leaders.

top.title: DOJ admits ICE arrested immigrants at courts under wrong legal authority
top.description: U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton said the government's defense of immigration courthouse arrests was based on a memo that only applied to criminal courthouses.

top.title: Iran claims missile strike on USS Abraham Lincoln as Gulf war widens
top.description: The IRGC launched attacks on Israel and U.S. bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain as drones set fire to a fuel tank at Kuwait's airport.

top.title: Zelensky says US tied security guarantees to Ukraine ceding Donbas
top.description: In a Reuters interview, the Ukrainian president warned the conditional demand would compromise the security of Ukraine and Europe.

top.title: Senate GOP launches reconciliation bill for Iran war, ICE and voter ID
top.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham said his Budget Committee will "expeditiously" draft a second party-line bill to bypass the filibuster, but key Republicans are skeptical.

top.title: Six Arab states demand Iraq halt militia attacks from its territory
top.description: Baghdad summoned the U.S. envoy and vowed a UN complaint after a deadly airstrike on the Habbaniyah base killed seven soldiers.

top.title: Pew poll finds 61% of Americans disapprove of Trump's Iran war
top.description: The survey of 3,524 adults, conducted March 16-22, also found 59% believe the U.S. made the wrong decision to use military force against Tehran.

top.title: US and Israel temporarily spare two Iranian leaders to allow peace talks
top.description: Ghalibaf and Araghchi will not be targeted for four to five days as mediators push for ceasefire negotiations amid the ongoing conflict.

top.title: Iran says it now controls Strait of Hormuz transit rules permanently
top.description: Tehran told the UN that only "non-hostile" ships may pass through the waterway carrying one-fifth of global oil, barring U.S. and Israeli vessels.

top.title: White House rejects Musk's offer to pay TSA workers
top.description: The rejection came as the DHS shutdown hit day 40, with a TSA official telling Congress that wait times have reached historic highs.

top.title: JetBlue shares surge on report it hired advisers to explore sale
top.description: The struggling airline is reportedly evaluating potential deals with United Airlines, Alaska Airlines, or Southwest Airlines.

top.title: Iranian analyst warns Tehran ready to seize UAE, Bahrain coastlines
top.description: Morteza Simiari said on state TV that Iran's military has rehearsed coastal operations and will act if the U.S. "makes any mistake."

top.title: White House says congressional approval not needed for Iran strikes
top.description: Spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. postponed strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure after "productive conversations" but warned Trump is "prepared to unleash hell."

top.title: Think tank proposes $100K cap on Social Security benefits
top.description: The Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget's "Six Figure Limit" plan would trim payouts to wealthy retirees as the program faces insolvency by 2032.

top.title: Egypt offers to host Iran-US talks as Tehran rejects ceasefire plan
top.description: Foreign Minister Abdelatty called direct engagement between Washington and Tehran "the last opportunity" to avoid full-scale regional escalation.

top.title: Netanyahu orders 48-hour blitz on Iran amid ceasefire fears
top.description: Israel's PM directed commanders to destroy as much of Iran's weapons industry as possible before a potential U.S.-brokered ceasefire deal.

top.title: NASA climate scientist Kate Marvel quits, citing Trump's 'attacks on science'
top.description: Marvel said a year of funding uncertainty and dismantled programs left her unable to do meaningful work at the Goddard Institute for Space Studies.

top.title: TSA chief warns of airport closures as DHS shutdown hits Day 40
top.description: Acting administrator told Congress that 480 officers have quit, wait times have topped four hours, and nearly $1 billion in paychecks have been missed.

top.title: Israel hits Tehran naval research center, completes waves of strikes
top.description: Defense Minister Katz says Israel has dropped over 15,000 munitions on Iran since the war began on Feb. 28, four times last June's total.

top.title: Pentagon signs deals to quadruple missile output amid Iran war
top.description: Lockheed Martin, BAE Systems, and Honeywell Aerospace agreed to accelerate production of key weapons as the U.S. replenishes wartime stockpiles.

top.title: Trump sets May 14-15 for rescheduled Beijing summit with Xi
top.description: The White House announced firm dates for the postponed China trip, which was delayed by the ongoing U.S. and Israeli war against Iran.

top.title: Fed's operating losses fell to $19B in 2025 from $78B
top.description: The central bank's audited financial statements show interest expenses dropped sharply as rate cuts reduced what the Fed pays on bank reserves.

top.title: EPA waives summer smog rules on gasoline amid Iran war price surge
top.description: The emergency waiver allows E15 ethanol-blend fuel sales starting May 1, as the Strait of Hormuz closure drives gas prices sharply higher.

top.title: Iran bolsters defenses on Kharg Island ahead of possible US assault
top.description: Tehran has deployed MANPADs, troops, and anti-armor mines to the strategic oil hub as the Trump administration weighs a ground operation, CNN reports.

top.title: Meta, YouTube found negligent in social media addiction trial
top.description: A Los Angeles jury awarded $3 million in damages to a young woman who alleged the platforms' addictive design features caused her mental health struggles.

top.title: Indiana bars Piece by Piece Autism Centers from Medicaid
top.description: The provider collected $29 million in 2023 to treat just 84 patients by exploiting a billing loophole that allowed reimbursements of up to $640 per hour.

top.title: Iran rejects U.S. ceasefire plan, issues five demands
top.description: Tehran dismissed Washington's 15-point proposal as "excessive and disconnected from reality" and countered with its own conditions to end the war.

top.title: Supreme Court rules ISPs not liable for users' piracy
top.description: The unanimous decision reverses a jury verdict against Cox Communications in a lawsuit led by Sony Music over illegal downloads of more than 10,000 songs.

top.title: TSA chief tells Congress agency won't be ready for World Cup
top.description: Nearly 460 officers have quit and unpaid payroll nears $1 billion as the DHS shutdown enters its 39th day, the acting TSA administrator testified.

top.title: Iran signals preference for Vance as US negotiator, rejecting Witkoff and Kushner
top.description: Tehran conveyed through back channels that it views Vance as more inclined to end the war, citing a "deficit of trust" with prior negotiators.

top.title: US gas prices hit $3.98 as lawmakers push tax suspensions
top.description: The national average has surged more than $1 in under a month, driven by the Iran conflict, with Georgia suspending its gas tax and Congress weighing a federal pause.

top.title: Trump gets daily strike montage on Iran war, raising concerns
top.description: Officials told NBC News the curated two-minute videos emphasize U.S. successes while offering little detail on Iranian attacks or setbacks.

top.title: NASA confirms Artemis II is go for April 1 moon launch
top.description: Four astronauts are set to become the first humans to travel to the moon in over 50 years aboard a 10-day test flight.

top.title: Pentagon orders 82nd Airborne troops to Middle East amid Iran ceasefire push
top.description: The deployment of up to 3,000 paratroopers coincides with a 15-point U.S. ceasefire proposal sent to Tehran through Pakistani intermediaries.

top.title: US ships 30,000 barrels of fuel to Cuba's private sector
top.description: The Trump administration's dual strategy of blockading Cuba's government while supplying private businesses is gaining traction, according to Reuters.

top.title: Astronomers spot two planets forming around a young star like our sun
top.description: WISPIT 2 is only the second known system where two planets have been directly observed taking shape, offering a rare window into our solar system's origins.

top.title: New York Times accuses Pentagon of defying court order on press access
top.description: The paper filed a new motion Tuesday after the department closed its press corridor and imposed escort requirements days after a judge struck down its media policy.

top.title: Prosecutor admitted no crime evidence in DOJ probe of Fed's Powell
top.description: A newly unsealed transcript shows the lead prosecutor told a judge his office had no evidence of criminal wrongdoing by the Fed chair.

top.title: Senate Republicans block 9th TSA funding bid as shutdown nears 40 days
top.description: Travelers face hours-long waits at major airports as over 50,000 TSA officers work without pay during the DHS funding standoff.

top.title: US Army raises max enlistment age to 42, eases marijuana rules
top.description: Updated regulation published March 20 also drops the waiver requirement for a single marijuana possession conviction to speed up recruiting.

top.title: Israeli airstrike hits Christian town north of Beirut for first time
top.description: The strike on Sahel Alma marks a widening of Israel's air campaign into previously untouched areas of Lebanon amid the escalating war with Hezbollah.

top.title: SpaceX aims to file for record-setting IPO in the next two weeks
top.description: The rocket and satellite company is moving toward what could be the largest public offering in history, with a June listing as the target.

top.title: US assessing how to resume nuclear weapons testing
top.description: Undersecretary DiNanno told lawmakers the administration has made no decisions on a testing program but did not rule out underground explosive tests.

top.title: DHS shutdown delayed NTSB investigators probing deadly LaGuardia crash
top.description: NTSB Chair Jennifer Homendy said investigators waited hours in TSA lines, preventing the team from completing a full first day of work at the crash site.

top.title: Democrat flips Florida House seat in Trump's Mar-a-Lago district
top.description: Emily Gregory defeated Trump-endorsed Republican Jon Maples in a district the president carried by about 10 points in 2024.

top.title: US airstrikes kill 15 Iraq PMF fighters, including Anbar commander
top.description: The deadliest single strike since the US-Israeli war on Iran began also targeted PMF leader Falih al-Fayadh's residence in a separate attack.

top.title: Iran charges ships up to $2M for Hormuz passage
top.description: Some vessels have already paid the ad hoc fees, according to Bloomberg, as shipping through the strait remains near collapse amid the Iran war.

top.title: Senate blocks third Democratic bid to curb Trump's Iran war powers
top.description: The 47-53 vote marked the third time this month Republicans defeated a resolution demanding congressional authorization for the military campaign.

top.title: Netanyahu taps top aide Dermer to vet US-Iran deal
top.description: Ron Dermer's mission focuses on ensuring Iran's nuclear program is dismantled and its enriched uranium stockpile eliminated, according to Bloomberg.

top.title: Anduril, Palantir building Golden Dome missile shield software
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reported the two firms are part of a nine-company consortium developing the $185 billion antimissile system's command-and-control layer.

top.title: Denmark's left bloc wins election but falls short of majority
top.description: Centrist leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen emerges as kingmaker with 14 seats as weeks of coalition talks loom.

top.title: Death toll rises to 69 in Colombia military plane crash
top.description: A C-130 Hercules went down shortly after takeoff Monday in the Amazon jungle, marking one of Colombia's deadliest military air disasters.

top.title: Iran says drones struck Israeli defense sites, Ben Gurion Airport
top.description: Tuesday's claimed strikes targeted Rafael Advanced Defense Systems and Israel Aerospace Industries as Operation True Promise 4 enters its fourth week.

top.title: Judge orders Trump to unfreeze $3B in Chicago transit funds
top.description: A federal judge granted a temporary restraining order releasing frozen rail funding, calling the administration's selective enforcement "pretextual."

top.title: Macron urges Iran to negotiate in third call with Pezeshkian since war began
top.description: The French president demanded an end to "unacceptable" attacks and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as U.S. peace efforts intensify.

top.title: Iranian missile intercepted over Lebanon in first since regional war began
top.description: Lebanon also expelled Iran's ambassador-designate, ordering him to leave by March 29 after accusing Tehran of violating diplomatic norms.

top.title: Iran stops all gas exports to Turkey after South Pars strike
top.description: The halt follows Israel's March 18 attack on the world's largest natural gas field, cutting off about 14% of Turkey's supply.

top.title: Lebanon expels Iran's ambassador as Israel vows to occupy southern Lebanon
top.description: Beirut declared Tehran's envoy persona non grata on Tuesday as Israel announced plans to seize territory up to the Litani River.

top.title: Debris from intercepted missiles knocks out 7 Kuwait power lines
top.description: Kuwait's Defense Ministry reported intercepting 17 ballistic missiles and 13 drones in 24 hours as the country endures its worst sustained bombardment since the Iran war began.

top.title: Israel says it killed Quds Force operative in Beirut strike
top.description: The IDF and Shin Bet said a naval strike on Monday killed Muhammad Ali Kourani, who they described as an Iranian-directed operative planning attacks from Lebanon.

top.title: China presses Iran to start peace talks as Gulf war escalates
top.description: Wang Yi told counterpart Abbas Araghchi that "talking is always better than fighting," urging all sides to seize "every opportunity" for negotiations.

top.title: British troops down 14 drones in record night at Iraq base
top.description: RAF Regiment forces intercepted the largest single-night drone swarm since the Iran conflict began, as Tehran resumed attacks after an Eid lull.

top.title: Second projectile hits Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant in a week
top.description: Iran accused the U.S. and Israel of striking the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant on Tuesday, the second such incident since March 17.

top.title: Democrats reject GOP offer to end DHS shutdown, demand ICE reforms
top.description: Senate Republicans proposed funding 94% of the DHS budget, but Democrats said the plan lacks immigration enforcement changes they have required since January.

top.title: Rubio testifies at ex-roommate's secret Venezuela lobbying trial
top.description: The secretary of state became the first sitting Cabinet member to take the stand in a criminal trial since 1983.

top.title: Erdogan vows to keep Turkey out of Iran war's 'ring of fire'
top.description: On the 25th day of the US-Israeli air campaign against Iran, Turkey's president blamed Netanyahu and warned against traps to draw Ankara into the conflict.

top.title: Moldova declares 60-day energy emergency after Russian strikes sever key power line
top.description: Parliament voted 72-0 to impose the emergency after overnight drone strikes on Ukraine knocked out the line supplying up to 70% of Moldova's electricity.

top.title: NASA outlines moon strategy as Artemis II targets April launch
top.description: The agency is hosting a Washington event to detail how it plans to return astronauts to the lunar surface by 2028 despite ongoing delays.

top.title: Iran fires cluster bomb missile at Bnei Brak, injuring 9
top.description: Multiple Iranian missile waves struck Israeli cities on Tuesday as the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran entered its fourth week.

top.title: Saudi crown prince urges Trump to press for Iran regime's fall
top.description: Mohammed bin Salman has called the US-Israeli war a "historic chance" to reshape the Middle East as Gulf states edge toward open confrontation with Tehran.

top.title: Aid flotilla docks in Havana as US oil blockade deepens Cuba's crisis
top.description: The first vessel of the "Our America Convoy" delivered medical supplies, food, and solar panels, with two more ships expected this week.

top.title: CENTCOM says 9,000 targets hit in Iran campaign's first 24 days
top.description: Iran has escalated missile and drone attacks against Gulf states even as U.S. officials say Tehran's combat capability is in steep decline.

top.title: Israel renews Beirut bombardment, killing 3
top.description: Airstrikes hit a residential building in Bchamoun and seven locations across Beirut's southern suburbs after a two-day lull in attacks.

top.title: Modi, Trump stress keeping Strait of Hormuz open amid Iran war
top.description: Their first call since the Feb. 28 U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran focused on de-escalation and securing the vital oil shipping route.

top.title: Brazil court places Bolsonaro under house arrest on health grounds
top.description: Justice Alexandre de Moraes reversed months of denials, granting a 90-day house arrest after the ex-president was hospitalized with severe pneumonia.

top.title: Hezbollah's missile stockpile down to one-sixth of pre-war levels, Israel says
top.description: Israel's military estimates the Iranian-backed group retains between 11,000 and 13,000 missiles and rockets, down from roughly 75,000 before October 2023.

top.title: Iran war drives fertilizer costs higher as U.S. planting season nears
top.description: Surging nitrogen prices, supplier shortages, and Strait of Hormuz disruptions are compounding years of losses for American grain farmers.

top.title: NASA cancels Lunar Gateway, will build $20B moon base
top.description: Administrator Jared Isaacman said the agency will repurpose the orbital station's components for a permanent lunar surface base over seven years.

top.title: Iran toughens negotiation stance, presses US for major concessions
top.description: Tehran's Revolutionary Guards are driving a harder negotiating line, with senior sources saying Iran will refuse to discuss limits on its missile program.

top.title: Pilots warned of LaGuardia hazards before deadly crash, CNN review finds
top.description: A CNN review of two years of safety reports revealed repeated warnings about miscommunication and near-misses at the airport before Sunday's fatal collision.

top.title: EU delays proposal to permanently ban Russian oil imports
top.description: The European Commission cited "current geopolitical developments" as the Iran war roils global energy markets with crude prices above $100 a barrel.

top.title: Iran war oil crisis revives US renewable energy debate
top.description: The largest oil supply disruption in history has Democrats pushing clean energy legislation as gas prices top $3.70 a gallon.

top.title: Iran fears ceasefire talks are a trap to kill its remaining leaders
top.description: Tehran suspects face-to-face negotiations could be used to target parliament speaker Ghalibaf, one of few surviving senior officials.

top.title: Taliban frees American academic Dennis Coyle after over a year in detention
top.description: Coyle, a 64-year-old linguist held in near-solitary confinement since January 2025, was reunited with his family in Kabul on Eid al-Fitr.

top.title: Russia launches 16 Rassvet satellites to rival Starlink
top.description: Bureau 1440 deployed its first operational batch from Plesetsk Cosmodrome on March 23, targeting commercial broadband service by 2027.

top.title: Israel to occupy southern Lebanon up to Litani River, defence minister says
top.description: Defense Minister Katz declared Israel will seize a "defensive buffer" covering nearly a tenth of Lebanon, the first explicit statement of such intent.

top.title: Gallup: Only 28% of US workers say it's a good time to find a quality job
top.description: For the first time in Gallup's tracking, more American workers report struggling in their lives than thriving, as job market confidence hits a multiyear low.

top.title: China mapping ocean floor for submarine warfare, Reuters finds
top.description: A Reuters investigation tracked 42 Chinese research vessels systematically charting seabeds across the Pacific, Indian, and Arctic oceans over five years.

top.title: Saudi Arabia, UAE edge toward joining war on Iran
top.description: Riyadh opened King Fahd Air Base to U.S. forces while the UAE dismantled Iranian institutions in Dubai, the Wall Street Journal reported.

top.title: Sunken Soviet sub is leaking radioactive material, study finds
top.description: A new PNAS study confirms the Komsomolets submarine is releasing strontium, cesium, uranium, and plutonium into the Norwegian Sea from its corroding hull.

top.title: Magnitude 7.6 earthquake strikes near Tonga; no tsunami threat
top.description: The deep quake hit 153 km west of Neiafu on Tuesday with no immediate reports of damage or casualties, according to USGS.

top.title: AWS Bahrain region disrupted by drones for second time this month
top.description: Amazon confirmed drone activity knocked out its cloud computing region in Bahrain again, as the company urges customers to migrate workloads elsewhere.

top.title: ISS images of Russian missile strike on Kyiv verified by analysts
top.description: Photographs taken from the space station on Dec. 27 show ballistic missiles streaking toward Kyiv and air defense systems firing back.

top.title: Kalshi, Polymarket ban insider trading as senators target industry
top.description: The two largest prediction market platforms rushed to announce new guardrails after bipartisan legislation threatened to ban sports contracts.

top.title: Pentagon closes press workspace, moves reporters off-site after court loss
top.description: Three days after a judge ruled its press restrictions unconstitutional, the Defense Department is relocating journalists to an annex outside the building.

top.title: Senate confirms Mullin as homeland security secretary
top.description: The Oklahoma Republican replaces Kristi Noem and inherits a department partially shut down since mid-February over immigration enforcement disputes.

top.title: Judge appoints career prosecutor as NJ's US attorney
top.description: Robert Frazer's selection resolves a bitter fight between the judiciary and the Trump administration over control of the federal prosecution office.

top.title: Israel used Iran's own street cameras to track and kill Khamenei
top.description: An AP investigation details how Israeli intelligence hacked nearly all of Tehran's traffic cameras years ago, turning Iran's surveillance network into a targeting tool.

top.title: Jury awards $19.25M to woman who accused Cosby of 1972 assault
top.description: The verdict in Santa Monica is the largest civil judgment against the 88-year-old entertainer, who maintains any encounter was consensual.

top.title: China urges US, Israel to halt Middle East fighting, warns of 'vicious cycle'
top.description: Beijing's diplomatic push comes as Trump postpones threatened strikes on Iranian power plants for five days amid disputed peace talks with Tehran.

top.title: Toyota invests $1B in Kentucky, Indiana plants
top.description: The funding will prepare its Georgetown factory for a second battery electric vehicle and boost output of the Camry, RAV4 and Grand Highlander.

top.title: Russia and Vietnam sign deal to build Vietnam's first nuclear plant
top.description: The Ninh Thuan 1 plant, featuring two reactors with up to 1.2 gigawatts each, is expected to be operational after 2031.

top.title: Trump admin pays TotalEnergies $928M to exit offshore wind
top.description: The deal cancels two East Coast wind leases and redirects the funds into LNG and oil production, marking a new tactic after courts blocked project suspensions.

top.title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to take effect provisionally May 1
top.description: The European Commission announced the provisional start date for the landmark pact, which will eliminate tariffs on over 90% of trade between the two blocs.

top.title: Both pilots killed as jet hits fire truck at LaGuardia
top.description: Air traffic control audio reveals a controller frantically shouting "stop" before an Air Canada Express plane struck a fire truck on the runway.

top.title: Trump tells ICE agents to drop masks at airports as deployment begins
top.description: ICE agents arrived at 14 major U.S. airports Monday to ease TSA staffing shortages caused by the five-week partial DHS shutdown.

top.title: Wright calls oil disruptions 'temporary,' urges producers to boost output
top.description: At CERAWeek, the energy secretary said prices haven't risen enough to curb demand as the U.S. ramps up its historic SPR drawdown.

top.title: Newark Airport grounds flights after smoke forces tower evacuation
top.description: An elevator malfunction caused a burning smell that led controllers to evacuate to a backup facility, halting arrivals and departures.

top.title: Sinopec says it won't buy Iranian crude despite US sanctions waiver
top.description: China's largest refiner is cutting runs by 5% and pushing Beijing to release state oil reserves as the Strait of Hormuz crisis squeezes supply.

top.title: Zelensky warns Patriot missile deficit is inevitable as Iran war drains stockpiles
top.description: The Ukrainian president said 803 interceptors were used on the first day of the Middle East war — more than the U.S. produces in a year.

top.title: Supreme Court hears case that could end mail ballot grace periods in 14-plus states
top.description: Justices weighed whether federal law bars states from counting mail ballots that arrive after Election Day, with a ruling expected by summer.

top.title: IAEA chief says no war can end Iran's nuclear program
top.description: Rafael Grossi told CBS News that Iran retains the knowledge and capacity to restart enrichment despite extensive U.S.-Israeli strikes.

top.title: WMO says Earth's climate 'more out of balance' than ever
top.description: The agency's annual report finds 2015-2025 was the hottest 11-year stretch on record, with energy imbalance at a 65-year high.

top.title: Bessent defends easing Russia, Iran oil sanctions on Meet the Press
top.description: Treasury Secretary said Russia would gain at most $2 billion from the waiver — or face $150-per-barrel oil prices without it.

top.title: Cuba says its military is preparing for possible U.S. aggression
top.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Carlos Fernandez de Cossio said regime change is "absolutely" off the table despite Trump's talk of "taking" the island.

top.title: Mossad plan to spark Iran uprising stalls three weeks into war
top.description: Intelligence chief Barnea promised Netanyahu that Israeli operations could trigger regime-toppling unrest, but no uprising has materialized.

top.title: Zelenskyy demands tighter Russia sanctions as Florida peace talks enter day two
top.description: Ukraine's president cited 1,550 drone strikes in one week, saying eased oil sanctions fund Russia's war as bilateral talks with the U.S. continue.

top.title: Trump draws bipartisan rebuke for celebrating Mueller's death
top.description: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director and special counsel in the Trump-Russia probe, died Friday at 81. Trump said he was "glad" on Truth Social.

top.title: IAEA says no radiation leaks after strikes near nuclear sites in Iran, Israel
top.description: Tit-for-tat attacks hit near Natanz and Dimona on day 23 of the war, with IAEA chief Grossi urging "maximum military restraint."

top.title: Senate blocks transgender sports ban amendment to voting bill
top.description: The 49-41 party-line vote rejected a GOP push to attach the ban to the SAVE America Act during a rare weekend session.

top.title: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in March as US oil blockade tightens
top.description: The grid collapse left millions without power as aging infrastructure buckles under fuel shortages caused by the Trump administration's economic pressure campaign.

top.title: US envoy calls Miami talks with Ukraine 'constructive' amid deadly attacks
top.description: Special Envoy Steve Witkoff said discussions with Ukrainian officials focused on narrowing gaps toward a peace deal as both sides traded fire Saturday.

top.title: G7 demands Iran halt attacks, vows to protect energy supplies
top.description: The joint statement condemned strikes across eight Middle Eastern nations and backed the right of targeted countries to defend themselves.

top.title: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel, dies at 81
top.description: Mueller reshaped the FBI after 9/11 and led the investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election during Trump's first term.

top.title: US quietly lifts sanctions on Russian nationals in fourth wave this month
top.description: The Treasury Department removed two Russian citizens and several other entities from its sanctions list on March 20, offering no explanation.

top.title: UK expands US base access to strike Iran's Strait of Hormuz missile sites
top.description: Starmer had initially limited US use of British bases to defensive measures but broadened the terms Friday to include protecting commercial shipping.

top.title: Indian, Asian refiners move to buy Iranian oil after US sanctions waiver
top.description: The 30-day exemption covers roughly 170 million barrels of Iranian crude already at sea, the third such waiver in two weeks.

top.title: Democrats conduct informal vote counts on ousting Schumer
top.description: Sen. Chris Murphy revealed that Democratic lawmakers have tallied votes on removing Schumer as minority leader, though he said Schumer retains enough support to stay.

top.title: Israel launches new wave of strikes on Beirut targeting Hezbollah
top.description: Saturday's airstrikes follow evacuation warnings as the ground offensive in southern Lebanon widens into its third week.

top.title: Switzerland halts new arms exports to US over Iran war
top.description: Bern cited its neutrality obligations, saying no new weapons licenses have been issued to Washington since the conflict began Feb. 28.

top.title: Judge blocks Pentagon policy restricting press access
top.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sided with The New York Times, ruling that key parts of the credentialing restrictions imposed under Defense Secretary Hegseth are illegal.

top.title: Jury finds Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors
top.description: A San Francisco jury found that two of Musk's tweets during his 2022 Twitter acquisition constituted securities fraud, with damages likely in the billions.

top.title: Iran offers to let Japanese ships pass through Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran is ready to facilitate transit for Japanese vessels if Tokyo coordinates with Iran, as negotiations continue.

top.title: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia base
top.description: Tehran launched two IRBMs at the joint U.S.-U.K. Indian Ocean base, marking its first known attempt to strike a target roughly 2,500 miles away.

top.title: Hegseth faces backlash for invoking faith in Iran briefings
top.description: The defense secretary's explicitly Christian rhetoric at Pentagon briefings has drawn sharp criticism as the U.S. enters its third week of war with Iran.

top.title: Trump ally asked ICE to detain his ex in custody fight, NYT reports
top.description: Paolo Zampolli contacted a senior ICE official to have his Brazilian ex-girlfriend detained and deported, aiding his bid for custody of their son.

top.title: U.S. prosecutors open criminal probes into Colombia's President
top.description: Federal offices in Manhattan and Brooklyn are examining whether Petro met with drug traffickers or received narcotics-linked campaign donations.

top.title: Pentagon rushes Marines, warships to Middle East as Iran war widens
top.description: The USS Boxer group and about 8,000 additional service members are headed to the region as the U.S. weighs further operations, including along Iran's coastline.

top.title: Amazon developing AI-powered smartphone a decade after Fire Phone flop
top.description: The project, codenamed "Transformer," would center on Alexa and AI-driven interactions rather than competing on hardware specs with Apple and Samsung.

top.title: Trump privately concedes deportation push went too far, WSJ reports
top.description: The president directed aides to rebrand the effort around arresting "bad guys" as polls show most Americans think enforcement has gone too far.

top.title: US launches air campaign to reopen Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Warplanes and attack helicopters are targeting Iranian naval assets as daily shipping traffic through the vital energy corridor has dropped roughly 95%.

top.title: Old-growth forests store far more carbon than logged ones, study finds
top.description: A decade-long study published in Science reveals Sweden's primary forests hold 83% more carbon than managed woodlands, driven largely by overlooked soil reserves.

top.title: US military readies Guantanamo Bay camp for potential Cuban exodus
top.description: DeSantis warned of mass migration to Florida as the SOUTHCOM commander told Congress the military has plans to handle refugees at Guantanamo Bay.

top.title: Denmark planned to destroy Greenland runways if US invaded
top.description: Danish broadcaster DR revealed that troops deployed to Greenland in January carried explosives to sabotage airstrips, with orders to fight if the U.S. attacked.

top.title: DOJ sues Harvard over alleged antisemitism on campus
top.description: The lawsuit seeks billions in taxpayer funds back and accuses the university of being "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students.

top.title: Alibaba, Tencent shed $66 billion as AI plans fail to convince investors
top.description: China's two largest tech companies lost a combined $66 billion in market value in roughly 24 hours after disappointing earnings and costly AI spending plans.

top.title: US bars Cuba, North Korea from Russia oil sanctions waiver
top.description: OFAC amended its temporary Russian oil license after a tanker reportedly delivered diesel to fuel-starved Cuba using deceptive shipping practices.

top.title: EU leaders endorse tax cuts, ETS overhaul to fight energy price surge
top.description: Von der Leyen pledged "temporary, tailored, and targeted" relief as the bloc grapples with soaring costs tied to the war in Iran.

top.title: Live Nation CEO admits pay tied to 'solving' DOJ antitrust case
top.description: Michael Rapino testified Thursday that about 90% of his compensation depends on profit targets and resolving the federal government's antitrust challenge.

top.title: Sailor's Strava jog exposes French carrier's location amid Iran conflict
top.description: Le Monde revealed that a naval officer's public fitness data pinpointed the Charles de Gaulle near Cyprus during its eastern Mediterranean deployment.
